--- PAGE 1 --- Housing Authority of the City of College Park
Board Minutes
Regular Monthly Meeting
May 28, 2020

Present: Chairperson, Robert Catlin;
Vice Chairperson, Arelis Perez;
Commissioners, Theresa Keeler,
betsy Lynn, and James McFadden;
Executive Director, Michelle
Johnson;

I. Roll Call:

Ms. Perez opened the meeting at 10:00 a.m. All Commissioners were present.

I. Agenda Changes:

Ms. Perez requested the following items be added to the agenda, under old business i.e.
- RFP

- Bank account switch

- PAsystem

- Call center

- Website

- Elevator

- Resident Council

- Account balance

- Parking lot

- Leases

- Garden

~ Review and Approval of Financial Report:
The Board reviewed/discussed the financial statements and bank statements. A motion was made by
Mr. Catlin and seconded by Ms. Lynn to approve the financial report, as submitted. Robert Catlin,
Theresa Keeler, Betsy Lynn, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez voted for the motion. The motion was
unanimously approved by a vote of 5-0.

Vil. Decision & Approval of Minutes from the Regular Monthly meeting held on April 23, 2020:
The Board reviewed/discussed the minutes from the regular monthly meeting held on April 23, 2020.
A motion was made by Ms. Perez and seconded by Mr. Catlin to approve the minutes from the
regular monthly meeting held on April 23, 2020, as submitted. Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, Betsy
Lynn, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez voted for the motion. The motion was unanimously
approved by a vote of 5-0.

IX. Old Business:

None. The Board decided by consensus to table all items requested under agenda changes.

X. New Business:

1) Approval of invoices over $2,000:

None.

2) Trustee Change - VOYA:
The Board reviewed/discussed the needed trustee change of the VOYA account. A motion was made
by Ms. Perez and seconded by Mr. McFadden to approve of removing James Simpson as the trustee
of the VOYA account and assign the new executive director Michelle Johnson as the trustee of the
VOYA account. Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, Betsy Lynn, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez voted for
the motion. The motion was unanimously approved by a vote of 5-0.

XI. Resident Comments:
None.
